trade.summary: Cina is India's largest trade source but India runs a massive deficit. India exports iron ore, cotton, seafood, and chemical intermediates. Cina exports electronics, machinery, chemicals, and consumer goods. India has restricted Chinese apps and investment, and is pursuing import substitution to reduce dependency. Despite political tensions, trade has continued growing.
disputes: Himalayan border dispute (Galwan Valley clash 2020; ongoing standoffs)
Indian bans on Chinese apps (TikTok, etc.)
Indian concerns about Chinese infrastructure in Karakoram (Pakistan)

diplomatic.summary: Relations at their lowest point in decades following the 2020 Galwan Valley clash which killed 20 Indian and 4 Chinese soldiers. Extensive de-escalation talks led to partial disengagement at most friction points by late 2024 but fundamental territorial disputes remain. India has pursued strategic autonomy, deepening ties with Stati Uniti (Quad) and Western partners while maintaining some engagement with Cina.
recent_incidents: 2020 Galwan Valley clash (deadliest India-Cina clash since 1967)
Prolonged border standoff at multiple Himalayan locations
India banned 200+ Chinese apps including TikTok
India restricted Chinese investment in sensitive sectors

military.summary: Active military competition and occasional confrontation. Both maintain large forces along the Line of Actual Control. India is participating in Stati Uniti-led Quad exercises and deepening defense ties with the Stati Uniti, Francia, and others. No arms transfers between countries. Cina is arming Pakistan (India's other major rival).
incidents: Ongoing military standoffs at Depsang, Gogra-Hot Springs (partially resolved 2024)

financial.summary: India has severely restricted Chinese FDI since 2020. Chinese apps banned, Chinese companies barred from major government contracts. Pre-2020 Chinese investment in Indian startups has been frozen. India is actively diversifying supply chains away from Cina. Some trade finance through third-country intermediaries continues.

energy.summary: India imports Chinese solar panels and battery components. India is trying to reduce this dependency through domestic manufacturing. Limited direct energy trade. India does not import Chinese fossil fuels significantly.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation has been suspended or severely curtailed since 2020. Indian government discourages institutional partnerships with Cina. Individual academic contacts remain. Joint publications are declining.
